pi_items.source: setup_task.sh injects 'sub-999' as the contaminated participant.
task.json narrative directly translates to setup_task.sh logic of 'n_clicks = random.randint(15, 25)' to simulate a stuck touchscreen.
Python code execution of setup_task.sh logic confirms 'sub-999' results in a WSE of 587.
verifier.py and export_result.sh explicitly check this file path.
setup_task.sh dynamically generates 15 standard participants (sub-01 to sub-15).

privileged_info_summary: The task dynamically generates a synthetic SWM clickstream dataset. It explicitly establishes 15 real participants (sub-01 to sub-15) and 1 contaminated participant (sub-999) whose behavior is manipulated to simulate a 'stuck_touchscreen'. This results in a massive number of Within-Search Errors (WSE) for sub-999, specifically 587, which successfully exceeds the WSE > 100 threshold defined in the prompt. The agent is expected to output a JSON report to /home/ga/pebl/analysis/swm_report.json containing aggregated performance metrics.
